name: Zaire (1971–1997)

colors.symbolism: the country's vegetation, hope, and the African revolution
the wealth of the country and the sun of Africa
the blood of the martyrs of independence; the flame of revolution
the arm of the people, holding aloft the torch

description: A green field charged at the centre with a large yellow disc; within the disc, a brown forearm and hand grasping a flaming red torch with smoke rising.
history: Adopted on 21 November 1971 when President Mobutu Sese Seko renamed the Democratic Republic of the Congo as the Republic of Zaire as part of his 'Authenticite' programme. The torch-and-arm device echoed the emblem of the ruling single party (MPR, Mouvement Populaire de la Revolution) and replaced the previous blue, yellow-star, and red-stripe Congolese flag. It was used until the overthrow of Mobutu in May 1997, when the country reverted to the name Democratic Republic of the Congo and to a flag based on its 1960s design.

features.symbolism: the hope and unity of the Zairian revolution and the country's lush vegetation

features.role: yellow disc as background for the central emblem
features.symbolism: the wealth of Zaire; a stage for the torch and the arm of the people.

features.role: lit torch held by a forearm and hand within the disc
features.symbolism: the torch of authentic Zairian revolution and the awakening of the people; the flame is the spirit of national renewal under Mobutu's MPR (Mouvement Populaire de la Revolution).

features.role: muscular forearm and hand holding the torch within the central disc
features.symbolism: the strength of the Zairian people grasping their own revolutionary destiny.
